text: I arrive as startup CTO comparing code hosting and AI coding claims. My past: I need developer speed, but AI and security claims need proof before I stake my team on them.
My screen is Laptop 15 (1440x900, desktop).
Today I want to: Understand whether the site gives enough proof to investigate further.
My life goal is: Protect my startup runway by choosing developer infrastructure that compounds.
I have 100 life-years. Each meaningful action costs 1 month(s); each committed dollar costs 1 year(s).

I think: I see a polished promise about collaboration and AI, but the first screen is still mostly aspiration, not proof.
My reaction: My reaction is interested but guarded; I like the platform framing, yet I need security, adoption, and workflow evidence before caring much.
Idea: Put a CTO path up front with proof points for small teams: reliability, permissions, AI governance, and migration effort.
Life-cost decision: Spending 1 month(s) to explore is worth it only if the next page moves from claims to concrete evidence for protecting my startup runway.
Truth assessment: My honest judgment is that GitHub has earned one more look because the brand is credible, but this screen alone gives me low-confidence proof.
My impulse is to explore.
I think: I wonder if the app has enough live people for me, because I keep missing the moment.
I click "/Features/i" because it looks like the next natural thing.
